description: HASS concept progression map — Knowledge and Understanding, Foundation to Year 6. Includes History, Geography, Civics and Citizenship, and Economics and Business where applicable.
subject: HASS

standards.title: Family relationships
standards.description: the people in their family, where they were born and raised, and how they are related to each other
standards.y_goal: Students identify the people in their family, where they were born and raised, and how they are related to each other.

standards.title: Family celebrations and commemorations
standards.description: the celebrations and commemorations of significant events shared with their families and others
standards.y_goal: Students identify celebrations and commemorations of significant events shared with their families and others.

standards.title: Special places and care
standards.description: the features of familiar places they belong to, why some places are special and how places can be looked after
standards.y_goal: Students identify the features of familiar places they belong to, why some places are special and how places can be looked after.

standards.title: Country/Place significance
standards.description: the importance of Country/Place to First Nations Australians and the Country/Place on which the school is located
standards.y_goal: Students explain the importance of Country/Place to First Nations Australians and identify the Country/Place on which the school is located.

standards.title: Family structures and roles over time
standards.description: differences in family structures and roles today, and how these have changed or remained the same over time
standards.y_goal: Students explain differences in family structures and roles today, and how these have changed or remained the same over time.

standards.title: Daily life continuity and change
standards.description: continuity and change between aspects of their daily lives and their parents’ and grandparents’ childhoods
standards.y_goal: Students identify continuity and change between aspects of their daily lives and their parents’ and grandparents’ childhoods.

standards.title: Features of local places
standards.description: the natural, managed and constructed features of local places, and their location
standards.y_goal: Students identify the natural, managed and constructed features of local places, and their location.

standards.title: Changing and caring for places
standards.description: how places change and how they can be cared for by different groups including First Nations Australians
standards.y_goal: Students explain how places change and how they can be cared for by different groups including First Nations Australians.

standards.title: Local significance
standards.description: a local individual, group, place or building and the reasons for their importance, including social, cultural or spiritual significance
standards.y_goal: Students explain the importance of a local individual, group, place or building, including its social, cultural or spiritual significance.

standards.title: Technological change in everyday life
standards.description: how technological developments changed people’s lives at home, and in the ways they worked, travelled and communicated
standards.y_goal: Students explain how technological developments changed people’s lives at home, and in the ways they worked, travelled and communicated.

standards.title: Spatial representation and scale
standards.description: how places can be spatially represented in geographical divisions from local to regional to state/territory, and how people and places are interconnected across those scales
standards.y_goal: Students explain how places can be spatially represented from local to state or territory scale, and how people and places are interconnected across those scales.

standards.title: Local First Nations interconnections
standards.description: the interconnections of First Nations Australians to a local Country/Place
standards.y_goal: Students explain the interconnections of First Nations Australians to a local Country/Place.

standards.title: Local community change
standards.description: causes and effects of changes to the local community, and how people who may be from diverse backgrounds have contributed to these changes
standards.y_goal: Students explain causes and effects of changes to the local community, and how people from diverse backgrounds have contributed to these changes.

standards.title: Australian symbols and commemorations
standards.description: significant events, symbols and emblems that are important to Australia’s identity and diversity, and how they are celebrated, commemorated or recognised in Australia, including Australia Day, Anzac Day, NAIDOC Week, National Sorry Day, Easter, Christmas, and other religious and cultural festivals
standards.y_goal: Students explain significant events, symbols and emblems important to Australia’s identity and diversity, and how they are celebrated, commemorated or recognised.

standards.title: Australia and neighbouring regions
standards.description: the representation of contemporary Australia as states and territories, and as the Countries/Places of First Nations Australians prior to colonisation, and the locations of Australia’s neighbouring regions and countries
standards.y_goal: Students explain the representation of contemporary Australia and First Nations Countries/Places, and identify the locations of Australia’s neighbouring regions and countries.

standards.title: First Nations connections across Australia
standards.description: the ways First Nations Australians in different parts of Australia are interconnected with Country/Place
standards.y_goal: Students explain the ways First Nations Australians in different parts of Australia are interconnected with Country/Place.

standards.title: Comparing places in the region
standards.description: the similarities and differences between places in Australia and neighbouring countries in terms of their natural, managed and constructed features
standards.y_goal: Students compare places in Australia and neighbouring countries in terms of their natural, managed and constructed features.

standards.title: Rules in communities
standards.description: who makes rules, why rules are important in the school and/or the local community, and the consequences of rules not being followed
standards.y_goal: Students explain who makes rules, why rules are important in the school and/or the local community, and the consequences of rules not being followed.

standards.title: Community participation
standards.description: why people participate within communities and how students can actively participate and contribute to communities
standards.y_goal: Students explain why people participate within communities and how students can actively participate and contribute to communities.

standards.title: First Nations diversity and continuity
standards.description: the diversity of First Nations Australians, their social organisation and their continuous connection to Country/Place
standards.y_goal: Students explain the diversity of First Nations Australians, their social organisation and their continuous connection to Country/Place.

standards.title: Causes of the first British colony
standards.description: the causes of the establishment of the first British colony in Australia in 1788
standards.y_goal: Students explain the causes of the establishment of the first British colony in Australia in 1788.

standards.title: Experiences of colonisation
standards.description: the experiences of individuals and groups, including military and civilian officials, and convicts involved in the establishment of the first British colony
standards.y_goal: Students explain the experiences of individuals and groups involved in the establishment of the first British colony.

standards.title: Effects of contact and invasion
standards.description: the effects of contact with other people on First Nations Australians and their Countries/Places following the arrival of the First Fleet and how this was viewed by First Nations Australians as an invasion
standards.y_goal: Students explain the effects of contact following the arrival of the First Fleet, and how this was viewed by First Nations Australians as an invasion.

standards.title: Importance of environments
standards.description: the importance of environments, including natural vegetation and water sources, to people and animals in Australia and on another continent
standards.y_goal: Students explain the importance of environments, including natural vegetation and water sources, to people and animals in Australia and on another continent.

standards.title: Resource management and sustainability
standards.description: sustainable use and management of renewable and non-renewable resources, including the custodial responsibility First Nations Australians have for Country/Place
standards.y_goal: Students explain the sustainable use and management of renewable and non-renewable resources, including First Nations Australians’ custodial responsibility for Country/Place.

standards.title: Rules and laws
standards.description: the differences between “rules” and “laws”, why laws are important and how they affect the lives of people
standards.y_goal: Students explain the differences between rules and laws, why laws are important and how they affect people's lives.

standards.title: Local government and services
standards.description: the roles of local government and how members of the community use and contribute to local services
standards.y_goal: Students explain the roles of local government and how members of the community use and contribute to local services.

standards.title: Community identity and diversity
standards.description: diversity of cultural, religious and/or social groups to which they and others in the community belong, and their importance to identity
standards.y_goal: Students explain the diversity of cultural, religious and/or social groups in the community and their importance to identity.

standards.title: Causes of British colonies after 1800
standards.description: the economic, political and social causes of the establishment of British colonies in Australia after 1800
standards.y_goal: Students explain the economic, political and social causes of the establishment of British colonies in Australia after 1800.

standards.title: Impacts of colonial development
standards.description: the impact of the development of British colonies in Australia on the lives of First Nations Australians, the colonists and convicts, and on the natural environment
standards.y_goal: Students explain the impact of the development of British colonies in Australia on First Nations Australians, colonists, convicts and the natural environment.

standards.title: Individuals and groups in colonial development
standards.description: the role of a significant individual or group, including First Nations Australians and those who migrated to Australia, in the development of events in an Australian colony
standards.y_goal: Students explain the role of a significant individual or group in the development of events in an Australian colony.

standards.title: People shape places
standards.description: the influence of people, including First Nations Australians and people in other countries, on the characteristics of a place
standards.y_goal: Students explain the influence of people, including First Nations Australians and people in other countries, on the characteristics of a place.

standards.title: Managing environments and hazards
standards.description: the management of Australian environments, including managing severe weather events such as bushfires, floods, droughts or cyclones, and their consequences
standards.y_goal: Students explain how Australian environments are managed, including the management of severe weather events and their consequences.

standards.title: Democracy, elections and representatives
standards.description: the key values and features of Australia’s democracy, including elections, and the roles and responsibilities of elected representatives
standards.y_goal: Students explain the key values and features of Australia’s democracy, including elections, and the roles and responsibilities of elected representatives.

standards.title: Citizens working for civic goals
standards.description: how citizens (members of communities) with shared beliefs and values work together to achieve a civic goal
standards.y_goal: Students explain how citizens with shared beliefs and values work together to achieve a civic goal.

standards.title: Resources, needs and wants
standards.description: types of resources, including natural, human and capital, and how they satisfy needs and wants
standards.y_goal: Students explain different types of resources, including natural, human and capital, and how they satisfy needs and wants.

standards.title: Federation and democracy
standards.description: significant individuals, events and ideas that led to Australia’s Federation, the Constitution and democratic system of government
standards.y_goal: Students explain significant individuals, events and ideas that led to Australia’s Federation, the Constitution and democratic system of government.

standards.title: Citizenship and political change
standards.description: changes in Australia's political system and to Australian citizenship after Federation and throughout the 20th century that impacted First Nations Australians, migrants, women and children
standards.y_goal: Students explain changes in Australia's political system and citizenship after Federation that impacted First Nations Australians, migrants, women and children.

standards.title: Migration and Australian society
standards.description: the motivation of people migrating to Australia since Federation and throughout the 20th century, their stories and effects on Australian society, including migrants from the Asia region
standards.y_goal: Students explain why people migrated to Australia since Federation, their stories, and their effects on Australian society.

standards.title: Asia region diversity and location
standards.description: the geographical diversity and location of places in the Asia region, and its location in relation to Australia
standards.y_goal: Students explain the geographical diversity and location of places in the Asia region, and its location in relation to Australia.

standards.title: Australia's international interconnections
standards.description: Australia’s interconnections with other countries and how these change people and places
standards.y_goal: Students explain Australia’s interconnections with other countries and how these change people and places.

standards.title: Government institutions and democracy
standards.description: the key institutions of Australia’s system of government, how it is based on the Westminster system, and the key values and beliefs of Western democracies
standards.y_goal: Students explain the key institutions of Australia’s system of government, how it is based on the Westminster system, and the key values and beliefs of Western democracies.

standards.title: Roles of government
standards.description: the roles and responsibilities of the 3 levels of government in Australia
standards.y_goal: Students explain the roles and responsibilities of the 3 levels of government in Australia.

standards.title: Consumer and financial choices
standards.description: influences on consumer choices and strategies that can be used to help make informed personal consumer and financial choices
standards.y_goal: Students explain influences on consumer choices and strategies for making informed personal consumer and financial choices.

progression_threads.description: Concept progression threads for HASS Knowledge and Understanding, organised by integrated strands across Foundation to Year 6.
